text: Do not be afraid, for I am with you. Do not be anxious, for I am your God. I will fortify you, yes, I will help you, I will really hold on to you with my right hand of righteousness.

Do not be anxious over anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication along with thanksgiving, let your petitions be made known to God; and the peace of God that surpasses all understanding will guard your hearts and your mental powers by means of Christ Jesus.
Jehovah is close to the brokenhearted; he saves those who are crushed in spirit.

Come to me, all you who are toiling and loaded down, and I will refresh you. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am mild-tempered and lowly in heart, and you will find refreshment for yourselves. For my yoke is kindly, and my load is light.
Trust in Jehovah with all your heart, and do not rely on your own understanding. In all your ways take notice of him, and he will make your paths straight.
It is through Jehovah's loyal love that we are not consumed, for his mercies never end. They are new every morning. Your faithfulness is great.
And he will wipe out every tear from their eyes, and death will be no more, neither will mourning nor outcry nor pain be anymore. The former things have passed away.
But those hoping in Jehovah will regain power. They will soar on wings like eagles. They will run and not grow weary; they will walk and not tire out.
God is our refuge and strength, a help that is readily found in times of distress.
We know that God makes all his works cooperate together for the good of those who love God, those who are the ones called according to his purpose.

Consider it all joy, my brothers, when you meet with various trials, knowing that this tested quality of your faith produces endurance. But let endurance complete its work, so that you may be complete and sound in all respects, not lacking in anything.
Cast all your anxiety on him, because he cares for you.
Find exquisite delight in Jehovah, and he will grant you the desires of your heart.
When you pass through the waters, I will be with you, and through the rivers, they will not flood over you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be scorched, nor will the flame singe you.
Jehovah is my Shepherd. I will lack nothing. In grassy pastures he makes me lie down; he leads me to well-watered resting-places. He refreshes me.
Have I not commanded you? Be courageous and strong. Do not be struck with terror or fear, for Jehovah your God is with you wherever you go.
For all things I have the strength through the one who gives me power.
Throw your burden on Jehovah, and he will sustain you. Never will he allow the righteous one to fall.
For I well know the thoughts that I am thinking toward you, declares Jehovah, thoughts of peace, and not of calamity, to give you a future and a hope.
You will safeguard those who have steadfast trust in you; you will grant peace because of their trust in you.
Jehovah is my light and my salvation. Whom should I fear? Jehovah is the stronghold of my life. Of whom should I be terrified?
A joyful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit saps one's strength.
I raise my eyes to the mountains. From where will my help come? My help comes from Jehovah, the Maker of heaven and earth.
Also, let the peace of the Christ rule in your hearts, for you were called to that peace in one body. And show yourselves thankful.
Keep on, then, seeking first the Kingdom and his righteousness, and all these other things will be added to you. So never be anxious about the next day, for the next day will have its own anxieties.
I keep Jehovah before me constantly. Because he is at my right hand, I will never be shaken.
In keeping calm and showing trust, your strength will be.
When anxieties overwhelmed me, your consolations soothed my soul.
May the God who gives hope fill you with all joy and peace by your trusting in him, so that you may abound in hope with power of holy spirit.
This is the day that Jehovah has made. We will be joyful and rejoice in it.
Jehovah your God is in your midst; as a mighty one, he will save you. He will rejoice over you with great joy. He will become silent in his love. He will be joyful over you with happy cries.
I will never leave you, and I will never abandon you. So that we may be of good courage and say: 'Jehovah is my helper; I will not be afraid.'
Why are you in despair, O my soul? Why are you agitated within me? Wait for God, for I will yet praise him as my Grand Savior.

But he said to me: 'My undeserved kindness is sufficient for you, for my power is being made perfect in weakness.' Most gladly, therefore, I will boast about my weaknesses, that the power of the Christ may remain over me like a tent.
I praise you because in an awe-inspiring way I am wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful; I know this very well.
Because you have been precious in my eyes, you have been honored, and I have loved you.
Trust in him at all times, O people. Pour out your hearts before him. God is a refuge for us.
Strength and splendor are her clothing, and she is cheerful about the future.
I will say to Jehovah: 'You are my refuge and my stronghold, my God in whom I trust.'
Be courageous and strong. Do not be afraid or dismayed. Jehovah God, my God, is with you. He will not forsake you or abandon you.

Jehovah will guide you constantly and satisfy you even in scorched places. He will strengthen your bones, and you will become like a well-watered garden and like a spring of water that never runs dry.
So let us not give up in doing what is fine, for in due time we will reap if we do not tire out.
Those who sow with tears will reap with a joyful cry.
Whatever you are doing, work at it whole-souled as for Jehovah, and not for men.

Do you not know? Have you not heard? Jehovah, the Creator of the ends of the earth, does not tire out or grow weary. His understanding is unsearchable. He gives power to the tired one and full might to those lacking strength.
Jehovah guides the steps of a man when He finds delight in his way. Even if he falls, he will not be hurled down, for Jehovah supports him by the hand.
Draw close to God, and he will draw close to you.
I earnestly hoped in Jehovah, and he inclined his ear to me and heard my cry for help. He brought me up out of a roaring pit, out of the miry clay.
Happy are those conscious of their spiritual need, since the Kingdom of the heavens belongs to them.

More than that, let us rejoice while in tribulations, since we know that tribulation produces endurance; endurance, in turn, an approved condition; the approved condition, in turn, hope, and the hope does not lead to disappointment.
Commit your way to Jehovah; trust in him, and he will act in your behalf.
Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.
My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the rock of my heart and my portion forever.
But you, O Jehovah, are a shield around me, my glory and the one who lifts up my head.
I will lie down and sleep in peace, for you alone, O Jehovah, make me dwell in security.
Jehovah will become a refuge for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of distress.
Those knowing your name will trust in you, for you have not abandoned those seeking you, O Jehovah.
Jehovah is my crag and my stronghold and the one who rescues me. My God is my rock, in whom I take refuge, my shield and my horn of salvation, my secure refuge.
You light my lamp, O Jehovah. My God brightens my darkness.
God is the one who girds me with strength, and he makes my way blameless.
May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be pleasing to you, O Jehovah, my Rock and my Redeemer.
May Jehovah answer you in the day of your distress! May the name of the God of Jacob protect you!
For he has not despised nor loathed the affliction of the humble one. He has not hidden his face from him. When he cried to him for help, he heard.
Even though I walk through a valley of deep shadow, I will fear nothing bad, for you are with me; your rod and your staff comfort me.
Make me know your ways, O Jehovah; teach me your paths.
He guides the humble in what is right, and he teaches the humble his way.
One thing I have asked from Jehovah — it is what I seek — that I may dwell in the house of Jehovah all the days of my life, to behold the pleasantness of Jehovah and to look upon his temple.
Hope in Jehovah; be courageous and strong of heart. Yes, hope in Jehovah.
Jehovah is my strength and my shield; in him my heart has trusted, and I have been helped. My heart rejoices, and I praise him with my song.
Jehovah gives strength to his people; Jehovah blesses his people with peace.
For his anger is but for a moment, but his favor is for a lifetime. In the evening weeping may settle in, but in the morning there is a joyful cry.
Be courageous, and may your heart be strong, all you who are waiting for Jehovah.
You are my hiding place; you will preserve me from distress. You will surround me with joyful songs of deliverance.
I will instruct you and show you the way to go; with my eye upon you, I will give you advice.
Look! The eye of Jehovah watches over those fearing him, those waiting for his loyal love.
I sought Jehovah and he answered me, and he rescued me from all my fears.
The angel of Jehovah camps all around those who fear him, and he rescues them.
The righteous cry out, and Jehovah hears; he rescues them from all their troubles.
Many are the hardships of the righteous one, but Jehovah rescues him from them all.
How precious your loyal love is, O God! In the shadow of your wings the sons of men take refuge.
Keep silent before Jehovah and wait expectantly for him. Do not be upset by the one who makes his way prosperous.
I was once young and now I am old, but I have not seen anyone righteous abandoned, nor his children looking for bread.
The righteous will possess the earth, and they will live forever on it.
He put a new song in my mouth, praise to our God. Many will see it and be in awe, and they will trust in Jehovah.
Why am I in despair? Why is my soul in turmoil? I will put my hope in God, for I will yet praise him as my grand savior and my God.
Give in and acknowledge that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations; I will be exalted in the earth.
Create in me a pure heart, O God, and put a new and steadfast spirit within me.
The sacrifice pleasing to God is a broken spirit; a broken and crushed heart, O God, you will not reject.
Evening and morning and midday I am troubled and I groan, and he hears my voice.
Show me favor, O God, show me favor, for I take refuge in you. And in the shadow of your wings I take refuge until the danger passes.
My soul rests in God alone. My salvation comes from him.
Wait in silence for God, O my soul, because my hope comes from him.
O God, you are my God; I earnestly search for you. My soul thirsts for you and my body longs for you, like a parched and weary land with no water.
For your loyal love is better than life; my own lips will glorify you.
For you have been my helper, and in the shadow of your wings I shout joyfully.
My soul clings to you; your right hand keeps a firm hold on me.
O you who hear prayer, to you people of all sorts will come.
Praise to God, who has not rejected my prayer or withheld his loyal love from me!
Praised be Jehovah, who daily carries our load, the true God of our salvation.
For you are my hope, O Sovereign Lord Jehovah, my confidence from my youth.
Though you made me see many troubles, you will revive me again. And from earth's depths you will raise me up. You will increase my greatness and comfort me again.
For Jehovah God is a sun and a shield; he gives favor and glory. Jehovah will not withhold anything good from those walking in integrity.
I will hear what the true God Jehovah says, for he will speak peace to his people and to his loyal ones.
For you, O Jehovah, are good and ready to forgive; you abound in loyal love for all those who call on you.
In the day of my distress I will call on you, for you will answer me.
But you, O Jehovah, are a God merciful and compassionate, slow to anger and abundant in loyal love and truth.
Satisfy us with your loyal love in the morning, so that we may shout joyfully and rejoice all our days.
The one who dwells in the secret place of the Most High will lodge under the shadow of the Almighty.
He will cover you with his feathers, and under his wings you will take refuge. His faithfulness will be a large shield and a protective wall.
For he will give his angels a command concerning you, to guard you in all your ways.
He will call on me and I will answer him. I will be with him in distress. I will rescue him and glorify him.
For you have made me rejoice, O Jehovah, because of your works. I shout joyfully over the works of your hands.
For Jehovah is good; his loyal love endures forever, and his faithfulness through all generations.
He will respond to the prayer of the destitute; he will not despise their prayer.
Praise Jehovah, O my soul, and do not forget all his acts of kindness — the one who forgives all your errors and heals all your ailments.
Jehovah is merciful and compassionate, slow to anger and abundant in loyal love.
For as the heavens are high above the earth, his loyal love toward those who fear him is as great.
As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed from us our transgressions.
As a father shows mercy to his sons, Jehovah has shown mercy to those who fear him.
But the loyal love of Jehovah is from everlasting to everlasting toward those who fear him.
Give thanks to Jehovah, for he is good; his loyal love endures forever.
For he has satisfied the thirsty soul and has filled the hungry soul with good things.
Light shines in the darkness for the upright; he is compassionate and merciful and righteous.
I love Jehovah because he hears my voice, my pleas for mercy.
Jehovah is compassionate and righteous; our God shows mercy.
Jehovah preserves the inexperienced; I was brought low, and he saved me.
Return to your rest, O my soul, for Jehovah has dealt kindly with you.
This is my comfort in my affliction, that your word has revived me.
May your loyal love comfort me, as you promised to your servant.
Your word is a lamp to my foot, and a light for my path.
You are my hiding place and my shield; I hope in your word.
Abundant peace belongs to those who love your law; nothing can make them stumble.
He will never allow your foot to slip. Your protector will never slumber.
Jehovah is your protector; Jehovah is your shade on your right hand.
Jehovah will protect you from every calamity; he will guard your life. Jehovah will guard you as you come and go, both now and forever.
Let Israel wait for Jehovah, for with Jehovah there is loyal love, and with him there is abundant redemption.
But I have calmed and quieted my soul like a weaned child on its mother; my soul is like a weaned child within me.
Though I walk in the midst of distress, you will preserve me alive. You will stretch out your hand against the anger of my enemies, and your right hand will save me.
Jehovah will carry out his purpose for me. Your loyal love, O Jehovah, endures forever. Do not abandon the works of your hands.
Let me hear your loyal love in the morning, for I trust in you. Show me the way I should walk, for to you I turn.
Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. May your good spirit lead me on level ground.
Jehovah is compassionate and merciful, slow to anger and great in loyal love.
Jehovah supports all who are falling and raises up all who are bowed down.
Jehovah is close to all those calling on him, to all who call on him in truth.
He satisfies the desire of those who fear him; he hears their cry for help, and he rescues them.
He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
Our Lord is great and is mighty in power; his understanding is beyond measure.
For Jehovah takes pleasure in his people. He adorns the humble with salvation.
For a child has been born to us, a son has been given to us; and the rulership will rest on his shoulder. His name will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Eternal Father, Prince of Peace.
Look! God is my salvation. I will trust and feel no dread, for Jah Jehovah is my strength and my might, and he has become my salvation.
Trust in Jehovah forever, for Jah Jehovah is the Rock of Ages.
But Jehovah is waiting to show you favor, and he will rise up to show you mercy. For Jehovah is a God of justice. Happy are all those keeping in expectation of him.
You will weep no more. He will surely show you favor at the sound of your cry for help; he will answer you as soon as he hears it.
O Jehovah, show us favor. We have hoped in you. Become our strength every morning, and save us in the time of distress.
Strengthen the weak hands, and make firm the knees that are shaking. Say to those with a fearful heart: 'Be strong. Do not be afraid. Look! Your own God will come. He will come and save you.'
And the ones Jehovah has redeemed will return and come to Zion with a joyful cry, and everlasting joy will crown their head.
Like a shepherd he will shepherd his own flock. With his arm he will gather the lambs together, and in his bosom he will carry them. He will gently lead those nursing their young.
For I, Jehovah your God, am grasping your right hand, the One saying to you, 'Do not be afraid. I will help you.'
I will lead the blind in a way they do not know, and I will make them walk in unfamiliar paths. I will make the darkness in front of them become light and the rough terrain level ground.
Do not be afraid, for I have redeemed you. I have called you by your name; you belong to me.
I will wipe out your transgressions like a cloud and your sins like a mist. Return to me, for I will repurchase you.
I, Jehovah, am your God, the One teaching you to benefit yourself, the One guiding you in the way you should walk.
Shout joyfully, you heavens, and rejoice, you earth. For Jehovah has comforted his people and will show his afflicted ones mercy.
Can a woman forget her nursing child or have no compassion for the son of her womb? Even if women were to forget, I would never forget you.
Look! I have engraved you on the palms of my hands; your walls are before me constantly.
For Jehovah will indeed comfort Zion. He will comfort all her devastated places, and he will make her wilderness like Eden and her desert plain like the garden of Jehovah.
The redeemed of Jehovah will return and come to Zion with a joyful cry. Everlasting joy will be upon their head.
But he was pierced for our transgression; he was crushed for our errors. The chastisement that brought us peace was upon him, and because of his wounds we have been healed.
Search for Jehovah while he may be found. Call to him while he is near.
For my thoughts are not your thoughts, and your ways are not my ways. For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so my ways are higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.
I reside in the high and holy place, but also with the crushed and lowly in spirit, to revive the spirit of the lowly and to revive the heart of the crushed ones.
The spirit of the Sovereign Lord Jehovah is upon me, because Jehovah anointed me to declare good news to the meek. He sent me to bind up the brokenhearted.
To provide for those mourning over Zion, to give them a crown instead of ashes, the oil of exultation instead of mourning, the garment of praise instead of a despondent spirit.
During all their distress it was distressing to him. The angel of his presence saved them. In his love and compassion he repurchased them, and he lifted them and carried them all the days of old.
And before they call, I will answer; while they are yet speaking, I will hear.
As a mother comforts her son, so I will keep comforting you; and in Jerusalem you will be comforted.
But the one listening to me will dwell in security and be undisturbed by the dread of calamity.
For Jehovah himself gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and discernment.
When you lie down, you will have no fear; you will lie down and your sleep will be pleasant.
But the path of the righteous is like the bright morning light that grows brighter and brighter until full day.
I love those who love me, and those who look for me will find me.
The blessing of Jehovah is what makes one rich, and He adds no pain with it.
The generous person will prosper, and whoever refreshes others will himself be refreshed.
Anxiety in a man's heart depresses him, but a good word cheers him up.
In the fear of Jehovah there is strong confidence, and it will be a refuge for his children.
Commit to Jehovah whatever you do, and your plans will succeed.
The one showing insight in a matter will find success, and happy is the one trusting in Jehovah.
The name of Jehovah is a strong tower. Into it the righteous one runs and receives protection.
Many are the plans in a man's heart, but the counsel of Jehovah is what will prevail.
For certainly there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
For the righteous one may fall seven times, and he will get up again, but the wicked will be made to stumble by calamity.
Trembling at men is a snare, but the one trusting in Jehovah will be protected.
Blessed is the man who puts his trust in Jehovah, whose confidence is in Jehovah.
Heal me, O Jehovah, and I will be healed. Save me, and I will be saved, for you are my praise.
From long ago Jehovah appeared to them and said: 'I have loved you with an everlasting love. That is why I have continued to show loyal love to you.'
I will turn their mourning into joy, and I will comfort them and give them joy instead of grief.
Call to me, and I will answer you and will tell you great things and hidden things that you have not known.
May Jehovah bless you and safeguard you. May Jehovah make his face shine upon you, and may he favor you. May Jehovah lift up his face toward you and grant you peace.
Be courageous and strong. Do not be afraid or suffer a shock before them, for Jehovah your God is the one marching with you. He will neither desert you nor abandon you.
Jehovah is the one marching before you, and he will continue with you. He will not forsake you, and he will not abandon you. Do not be afraid or be terrified.
The joy of Jehovah is your stronghold.
But he knows the way I take. After he has tested me, I will come out like gold.
But as for me, I will keep on the lookout for Jehovah. I will wait for the God of my salvation. My God will hear me.
Jehovah is good, a stronghold in the day of distress. He acknowledges those seeking refuge in him.
Jehovah the Sovereign Lord is my vital energy, and he will make my feet like those of a deer and cause me to tread on high places.
For the eyes of Jehovah are roving about through all the earth to show his strength in behalf of those whose heart is complete toward him.
For this is what the Sovereign Lord Jehovah says: Here I am, and I will search for my sheep and care for them.
Jehovah is good to those who wait for him, to the person who seeks him.
For even if he causes grief, he will show compassion according to his abundant loyal love.
You drew near on the day I called to you. You said: 'Do not be afraid.'
Happy are those who mourn, since they will be comforted.
Happy are those hungering and thirsting for righteousness, since they will be filled.
Happy are the pure in heart, since they will see God.
Happy are the peacemakers, since they will be called sons of God.
Keep on asking, and it will be given you; keep on seeking, and you will find; keep on knocking, and it will be opened to you.
Therefore, if you, although being wicked, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more so will your Father who is in the heavens give good things to those asking him!

Are not two sparrows sold for a coin of small value? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father's knowledge. Even the hairs of your head are all numbered. So have no fear; you are worth more than many sparrows.
For where there are two or three gathered together in my name, there I am in their midst.
With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.
All things are possible for the one who has faith.
This is why I tell you, all the things you pray and ask for, have faith that you have already received them, and you will have them.
For no declaration will be an impossibility with God.
Even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Have no fear; you are worth more than many sparrows.
Have no fear, little flock, for your Father has approved of giving you the Kingdom.
As these things start to occur, stand up straight and lift up your heads, because your deliverance is getting near.
For God loved the world so much that he gave his only-begotten Son, so that everyone exercising faith in him might not be destroyed but have everlasting life.
I came so that they might have life and have it in abundance.
My sheep listen to my voice, and I know them, and they follow me. I give them everlasting life, and they will by no means ever be destroyed.
I am the resurrection and the life. The one who exercises faith in me, even though he dies, will come to life.
Do not let your hearts be troubled. Exercise faith in God; exercise faith also in me.
In the house of my Father there are many dwelling places. Otherwise, I would have told you, for I am going my way to prepare a place for you.
I leave you peace; I give you my peace. I do not give it to you the way that the world gives it. Do not let your hearts be troubled nor let them shrink out of fear.
If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you wish and it will be done for you.
I have spoken these things to you so that my joy may be in you and your joy may be complete.
You, too, are now in grief, but I will see you again, and your hearts will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.
I have said these things to you so that by means of me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation, but take courage! I have conquered the world.
Therefore, now that we have been declared righteous as a result of faith, let us have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Through him we also have obtained access by faith into this undeserved kindness in which we now stand, and let us rejoice based on hope of the glory of God.
But God recommends his own love to us in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
Therefore, those in union with Christ Jesus have no condemnation.
For all who are led by God's spirit are indeed God's sons.
For I consider that the sufferings of the present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is about to be revealed in us.

In the same way, the spirit also joins in to help us in our weakness; for the problem is that we do not know what we should pray for as we need to, but the spirit itself pleads for us with unuttered groanings.
What, then, shall we say about these things? If God is for us, who will be against us?
On the contrary, in all these things we are coming off completely victorious through the one who loved us.

For I am convinced that neither death nor life nor angels nor governments nor things now here nor things to come nor powers nor height nor depth nor any other creation will be able to separate us from God's love that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
For the scripture says: 'No one who rests his faith on him will be disappointed.'
Rejoice in the hope. Endure under tribulation. Persevere in prayer.
For the Kingdom of God does not mean eating and drinking, but it means righteousness and peace and joy with holy spirit.
For his part, the God who gives peace will crush Satan under your feet shortly.
But just as it is written: 'Eye has not seen and ear has not heard, nor have there been conceived in the heart of man the things that God has prepared for those who love him.'

No temptation has come upon you except what is common to men. But God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear, but along with the temptation he will also make the way out so that you may be able to endure it.

Love is patient and kind. Love is not jealous. It does not brag, does not get puffed up, does not behave indecently, does not look for its own interests. It does not keep account of the injury. It does not rejoice over unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth. It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
Now, however, these three remain: faith, hope, love; but the greatest of these is love.
But thanks be to God, for he gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!
Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always having plenty to do in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor is not in vain in connection with the Lord.
Stay awake, stand firm in the faith, carry on as men, grow mighty.

Praised be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of tender mercies and the God of all comfort, who comforts us in all our trials so that we may be able to comfort others in any sort of trial.
We are hard-pressed in every way, but not cramped beyond movement; we are perplexed, but not absolutely with no way out; we are persecuted, but not abandoned; we are knocked down, but not destroyed.
Therefore, we do not give up, but even if the man we are outside is wasting away, certainly the man we are inside is being renewed from day to day.
For though the tribulation is momentary and light, it works out for us a glory that is of more and more surpassing weight and is everlasting.
Consequently, if anyone is in union with Christ, he is a new creation; the old things passed away; look! new things have come into existence.
For he says: 'In an acceptable time I heard you, and in a day of salvation I helped you.'
Moreover, God is able to cause all his undeserved kindness to abound toward you, so that you will always have all sufficiency in everything and have plenty for every good work.
Finally, brothers, continue to rejoice, to be restored, to be comforted, to think in agreement, to live peacefully; and the God of love and of peace will be with you.

I am nailed to the stake along with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ is living in me. Indeed, the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and handed himself over for me.
So you are no longer a slave but a son; and if a son, then also an heir through God.
For such freedom Christ set us free. Therefore, stand firm, and do not be loaded again with a yoke of slavery.
On the other hand, the fruitage of the spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, mildness, self-control. Against such things there is no law.
By means of him we have the release by ransom through the blood of that one, yes, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his undeserved kindness.
By this undeserved kindness you have been saved through faith, and this is not of your own doing; rather, it is God's gift.
We are God's handiwork and were created in union with Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared in advance for us to walk in them.
I pray that he may grant you through the abundance of his glory to be made mighty in the man you are inside, with power through his spirit.

Through your faith Christ may dwell in your hearts with love. May you be rooted and established on the foundation, in order that you may be thoroughly able to grasp with all the holy ones what is the breadth and length and height and depth, and to know the love of the Christ.
Now to the one who can, according to his power that is operating in us, do more than superabundantly beyond all the things we ask or conceive.
But become kind to one another, tenderly compassionate, freely forgiving one another just as God also by Christ freely forgave you.
Finally, go on acquiring power in the Lord and in the mightiness of his strength.
For I am confident of this very thing, that the one who started a good work in you will bring it to completion until the day of Jesus Christ.
For God is the one who, for the sake of his good pleasure, is acting within you to will and also to act.
Always rejoice in the Lord. Again I will say, rejoice!
And the peace of God that surpasses all understanding will guard your hearts and your mental powers by means of Christ Jesus.

Whatever things are true, whatever things are of serious concern, whatever things are righteous, whatever things are chaste, whatever things are lovable, whatever things are well-spoken-of, whatever things are virtuous, and whatever things are praiseworthy, continue considering these things.
In turn, my God will fully supply all your need according to his riches in glory by means of Christ Jesus.
I am pressing on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God by means of Christ Jesus.
May you be strengthened with all power according to his glorious might so that you may endure fully and be patient.
Keep your minds fixed on the things above, not on the things on the earth.
Always be rejoicing. Pray constantly. Give thanks for everything. This is the will of God for you in union with Christ Jesus.
May the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your entire spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless at the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Moreover, may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and gave everlasting comfort and a good hope by his undeserved kindness, comfort your hearts and strengthen you in every good deed and word.
But the Lord is faithful, and he will strengthen you and protect you from the wicked one.
Of course, godly devotion along with contentment is a means of great gain.
For God did not give us a spirit of cowardice, but one of power and of love and of soundness of mind.
But the Lord stood near me and infused power into me, so that through me the preaching might be fully accomplished.
The Lord will rescue me from every wicked work and will save me for his heavenly Kingdom.
For we do not have a high priest who cannot sympathize with our weaknesses, but we have one who has been tested in all respects as we have, but without sin.
Let us, therefore, approach the throne of undeserved kindness with boldness so that we may receive mercy and find undeserved kindness to help us at the right time.
For God is not unrighteous so as to forget your work and the love you showed for his name by ministering to the holy ones.
Let us hold fast the public declaration of our hope without wavering, for the one who promised is faithful.
Faith is the assured expectation of what is hoped for, the evident demonstration of realities that are not seen.

As we look intently at the Chief Agent and Perfecter of our faith, Jesus. For the joy that was set before him he endured a torture stake, despising shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.
True, no discipline seems for the present to be joyful, but it is painful; yet afterward, it yields the peaceable fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
If any one of you is lacking in wisdom, let him keep asking God, for he gives generously to all and without reproaching, and it will be given him.
We consider happy those who have endured. You have heard of the endurance of Job and have seen the outcome Jehovah gave, that Jehovah is very tender in affection and merciful.
The supplication of a righteous person has great power and produces results.

But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for special possession, so that you should declare abroad the excellencies of the One who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.
For the eyes of Jehovah are on the righteous, and his ears listen to their supplication.
So, then, let those who suffer in harmony with the will of God keep on entrusting their souls to a faithful Creator while doing good.
Jehovah is not slow concerning his promise, as some people consider slowness, but he is patient with you because he does not desire anyone to be destroyed but desires all to attain to repentance.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous so as to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
See what sort of love the Father has given us! That we should be called children of God! And that is what we are.
You originate with God, little children, and you have conquered those men, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
Beloved ones, let us continue loving one another, because love is from God, and everyone who loves has been born from God and knows God.
There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts fear aside, because fear involves punishment.
And this is the confidence that we have toward him, that no matter what we ask according to his will, he hears us.
Look! I am standing at the door and knocking. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come into his house and take the evening meal with him and he with me.
For the Lamb, who is in the midst of the throne, will shepherd them and will guide them to springs of waters of life. And God will wipe out every tear from their eyes.
And the one seated on the throne said: 'Look! I am making all things new.'
O Jehovah, you hear my voice in the morning; in the morning I state my case to you, and I wait expectantly.
The desire of the meek you will hear, O Jehovah. You will strengthen their heart; you will cause your ear to hear.
May he grant you the desires of your heart and make all your plans succeed.
How abundant your goodness is, which you have stored up for those fearing you!
Jehovah redeems the life of his servants; none who take refuge in him will be found guilty.
For with you is the source of life; by your light we can see light.
But I am like a flourishing olive tree in God's house. I trust in God's loyal love forever and ever.
But God has heard; he has paid attention to my prayer.
Become for me a rock of refuge, a stronghold to give me salvation, for you are my crag and my stronghold.
O Jehovah God of armies, restore us. Make your face shine upon us, and we will be saved.
But I will not withdraw my loyal love from him, nor will I betray my faithfulness.
Light is sown for the righteous, and joy for the upright in heart.
For your loyal love is great, higher than the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
You who fear Jehovah, trust in Jehovah! He is their help and their shield.
I have been weeping from grief; strengthen me according to your word.
I have hoped in Jehovah; my soul has hoped, and I wait for his word.
I cried out to you, O Jehovah. I said: 'You are my refuge, my share in the land of the living.'
Jehovah is good to all, and his mercies are over all his works.
Jehovah favors those who fear him, those waiting for his loyal love.
You will not fear any sudden terror or the storm that is coming upon the wicked, for Jehovah will prove to be your source of confidence, and he will keep your foot from being caught.
Expectation postponed makes the heart sick, but a desire realized is a tree of life.
And I will give them a heart to know me, that I am Jehovah. They will become my people, and I will become their God, for they will return to me with their whole heart.
I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit within you.
I will engage you to me forever, and I will engage you to me in righteousness and in justice and in loyal love and in mercy.
I will make compensation to you for the years that the swarming locust, the unwinged locust, the voracious locust, and the cockroach ate your crops.
Test me out, please, in this regard, whether I will open to you the floodgates of the heavens and pour out on you a blessing until there is nothing lacking.
But he did not waver in faith regarding the promise of God; but he became powerful by his faith, giving God glory and being fully convinced that what He had promised He was also able to do.
Therefore, I appeal to you by the mercies of God, brothers, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, a sacred service with your power of reason.
Now Jehovah is the Spirit, and where the spirit of Jehovah is, there is freedom.
Not by a military force, nor by power, but by my spirit, says Jehovah of armies.
Whoever touches you touches the pupil of my eye.

They could seek God, if they would, in the hope that they might grope for him and really find him, although, in fact, he is not far off from each one of us. For by him we have life and move and exist.

How many things you have done, O Jehovah my God, your wonderful works and your thoughts toward us. None can compare to you; if I were to try to tell and speak of them, they would be too numerous to recount.
You are the true God, who does wonders. You have made known your strength among the peoples.
He brought me into the banquet hall, and his banner over me is love.
It is good to wait in silence for the salvation of Jehovah.
For this is what Jehovah says to the house of Israel: 'Search for me and live.'
Distress and anguish have overtaken me, but your commandments are my delight.
For with you there is forgiveness, so that you may be held in awe.
Every breathing thing — let it praise Jah. Praise Jah!
Surely goodness and loyal love will pursue me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of Jehovah for all my days.
The intimacy of Jehovah belongs to those who fear him, and he makes his covenant known to them.
Taste and see that Jehovah is good; happy is the man who takes refuge in him.
But the meek will possess the earth, and they will find exquisite delight in the abundance of peace.
When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
But I will keep hoping; I will add to all your praise.
He satisfies you with good things all your life, so that your youth is renewed just like the eagle's.
Jehovah is great and most worthy of praise; his greatness is beyond comprehension.
